behavioral_changes.summary: Daily trend entries now expose a `refreshPolicyDistribution` payload with documented semantics that its component totals represent per-run fractional refresh-policy contributions for the period.
The daily trend SQL now projects `fractionFull` as `SUM(COALESCE(fraction_full, 0))`, preventing legacy NULL source values from surfacing as a null full-refresh aggregate.
The daily trend SQL now projects `fractionIncremental` as `SUM(COALESCE(fraction_incremental, 0))`, preventing legacy NULL source values from surfacing as a null incremental-refresh aggregate.
The daily trend SQL now projects `fractionNoRefresh` as `SUM(COALESCE(fraction_no_refresh, 0))`, preventing legacy NULL source values from surfacing as a null no-refresh aggregate.
Trend-row mapping now treats a `DBNull` `run_date` as `null` instead of calling `GetString` on a missing value.
Trend-row mapping now defaults `RefreshPolicyDistribution.Full` to `0` when `fractionFull` is `DBNull`.
Trend-row mapping now defaults `RefreshPolicyDistribution.Incremental` to `0` when `fractionIncremental` is `DBNull`.
Trend-row mapping now defaults `RefreshPolicyDistribution.NoRefresh` to `0` when `fractionNoRefresh` is `DBNull`.
Trend-row mapping now rounds refresh-policy fraction values to 1 decimal place to match the summary endpoint precision contract.

grounding_evidence.reason: Adds the full-refresh fraction aggregate to the daily trend SQL and null-coalesces legacy NULL rows before summing.
Adds the incremental-refresh fraction aggregate to the daily trend SQL and null-coalesces legacy NULL rows before summing.
Adds the no-refresh fraction aggregate to the daily trend SQL and null-coalesces legacy NULL rows before summing.
Contract-bearing response documentation defines the new field's semantics and its sum-to-eligible-runs invariant.

grounding_evidence.excerpt:
[JsonProperty("refreshPolicyDistribution")]
public RefreshPolicyDistribution RefreshPolicyDistribution { get; set; } = new RefreshPolicyDistribution();
grounding_evidence.reason: Adds `refreshPolicyDistribution` to the serialized daily-trend DTO and initializes it non-null.
Routes daily trend row construction through the new mapper so the added refresh-policy payload participates in the response pipeline.
Introduces a defensive DBNull guard for `run_date`, mapping missing database values to null.
Maps `fractionFull` with both a DBNull-to-zero guard and 1-decimal rounding.
Maps `fractionIncremental` with both a DBNull-to-zero guard and 1-decimal rounding.
Maps `fractionNoRefresh` with both a DBNull-to-zero guard and 1-decimal rounding.
Contract-bearing documentation defines the new cross-endpoint precision invariant for trend fractions.
New day-grouping unit test locks the SQL builder to emit all three null-safe fraction aggregates.
New week-grouping unit test locks the SQL builder to emit the same null-safe fraction aggregates when grouping switches.
Happy-path mapping test locks the nested refresh-policy payload onto a populated daily trend row.
All-null mapping test locks default-to-zero behavior for every distribution component.
Partial-null mapping test proves only the missing incremental fraction is defaulted while present sibling values are preserved.
Unit test locks the new DBNull `run_date` => null mapping path.
Unit test locks 1-decimal rounding across the mapped refresh-policy fractions.

scenario_sketches.title: Daily trend rows emit `refreshPolicyDistribution` whose component totals represent the period's refresh-policy split
scenario_sketches.rationale: Exercise a populated trend row and assert the daily-trend DTO includes the new nested payload, with values flowing through the response pipeline and matching the documented per-period distribution contract.

scenario_sketches.title: Day and week trend queries both include a null-safe `fractionFull` aggregate
scenario_sketches.rationale: Parameterize `BuildDailyTrendQuery` over `day` and `week` and assert both outputs retain `SUM(COALESCE(fraction_full, 0)) AS fractionFull`, guarding legacy NULL source rows.

scenario_sketches.title: Day and week trend queries both include a null-safe `fractionIncremental` aggregate
scenario_sketches.rationale: Parameterize `BuildDailyTrendQuery` over `day` and `week` and assert both outputs retain `SUM(COALESCE(fraction_incremental, 0)) AS fractionIncremental`, guarding legacy NULL source rows.

scenario_sketches.title: Day and week trend queries both include a null-safe `fractionNoRefresh` aggregate
scenario_sketches.rationale: Parameterize `BuildDailyTrendQuery` over `day` and `week` and assert both outputs retain `SUM(COALESCE(fraction_no_refresh, 0)) AS fractionNoRefresh`, guarding legacy NULL source rows.

scenario_sketches.title: `MapTrendEntryAsync` maps a `DBNull` `run_date` to `null`
scenario_sketches.rationale: Drive the mapper with a row whose only missing scalar is `run_date` and assert the DTO returns `null` rather than attempting `GetString` on a database null.

scenario_sketches.title: `MapTrendEntryAsync` defaults `RefreshPolicyDistribution.Full` to `0.0` when `fractionFull` is `DBNull`
scenario_sketches.rationale: Use a row where `fractionFull` is absent and verify the Full component is zeroed instead of propagating database null into the DTO.

scenario_sketches.title: `MapTrendEntryAsync` defaults `RefreshPolicyDistribution.Incremental` to `0.0` when `fractionIncremental` is `DBNull`
scenario_sketches.rationale: Use a partially-null row to prove the Incremental component alone defaults to zero while present Full and NoRefresh values remain intact.

scenario_sketches.title: `MapTrendEntryAsync` defaults `RefreshPolicyDistribution.NoRefresh` to `0.0` when `fractionNoRefresh` is `DBNull`
scenario_sketches.rationale: Use a row where `fractionNoRefresh` is missing and verify the NoRefresh component is zeroed instead of surfacing a null-derived value.

scenario_sketches.title: Trend refresh-policy fractions are rounded to 1 decimal to match summary endpoint precision
scenario_sketches.rationale: Feed in high-precision fraction values around rounding boundaries and assert the mapped Full, Incremental, and NoRefresh components all round to one decimal consistently with the documented cross-endpoint contract.
